name: Matsumoto Shave Ice
tagline: The North Shore icon since 1951 — the rainbow combo is non-negotiable.
mustOrder: azuki beans + ice cream on the bottom
parking: Street parking — fills fast on weekends
talkStory: Mamoru and Helen Matsumoto opened this Haleiwa general store in 1951, originally selling groceries to plantation workers. The shave ice was just a side offering, but it became so popular that by the 1970s it had taken over the entire business. The original wooden building and hand-painted sign are now North Shore landmarks. Lines wrap around the building during peak season — 20 minutes on a good day, 45-60 minutes when tour buses arrive. Arrive right at 10am opening for the shortest wait. President Obama stopped here during Hawaii vacations, and the branded T-shirts have become a legitimate Oahu souvenir. Over a thousand customers per day during peak season.
